eventMetadata.context_description: **Federal AI safety legislation remains stalled in a divided 119th Congress, driving trader consensus to a 73% implied probability against enactment before 2027.** Despite bipartisan introductions like the AI Guardrails Act (S.4113) and Protect American AI Act (H.R.8037) in March 2026, no major bills have advanced past committees amid partisan gridlock, with Republicans prioritizing innovation and preemption of state laws per the Trump administration's March National Policy Framework, while Democrats push stronger safety measures. Recent state-level action surges—including chatbot disclosure laws in Nebraska and deepfake bans—highlight federal inaction, as Congress focuses on appropriations and debt ceiling talks. Upcoming lame-duck session offers slim passage odds without whip counts shifting dramatically.

markets.question: Will Yadira Caraveo be the Democratic nominee for CO-08?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Democratic Party to contest the CO-08 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Democratic primary will take place on June 30, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: CO-08 Democratic Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Democratic Party to contest the CO-08 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Democratic primary will take place on June 30, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices state Rep. Manny Rutinel at an 81% implied probability to win Colorado's 8th Congressional District Democratic primary on June 30, reflecting his fundraising lead and aggressive digital ad campaign attacking former Rep. Shannon Bird on immigration votes. A late-April internal poll showed Bird edging Rutinel 25%-24% amid 45% undecideds, confirming a two-candidate race as others like former incumbent Yadira Caraveo fade, but Rutinel's online dominance, retail momentum, and National Republican Congressional Committee attacks signaling general election viability have boosted his standing. With ballots mailing soon in this battleground against vulnerable GOP Rep. Gabe Evans, high undecideds leave room for shifts via spending and turnout.

markets.question: Will Abdul El-Sayed win the 2026 Michigan Democratic Primary?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for United States Senator from Michigan.

If no 2026 Michigan Democratic Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Michigan Democratic party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Michigan Democratic Senate Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for United States Senator from Michigan.

If no 2026 Michigan Democratic Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Michigan Democratic party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket gives Abdul El-Sayed a 50.5% implied probability of winning Michigan's Democratic Senate primary on August 4, ahead of Mallory McMorrow at 30.5% and Haley Stevens at 16.7%, reflecting his recent surge in polling averages and strength among voters under 40, where he leads McMorrow by 17 points per Emerson's April survey. Recent Emerson (April 11-13) and Glengariff (April 17-19) polls show a tight three-way race with El-Sayed and McMorrow tied near 24% or Stevens edging ahead at 25%, bolstered by first-quarter fundraising where El-Sayed and McMorrow outpaced Stevens. Stevens gained former Sen. Debbie Stabenow's endorsement and a $5 million pro-Israel group ad buy this week, yet traders prioritize El-Sayed's progressive appeal and youth turnout potential in the open-seat contest following Gary Peters' retirement.

markets.question: Will Germany be the Jury Winner in the Eurovision 2026 Grand Final?

The Grand Final of the Eurovision Song Contest 2026 is scheduled for May 16, 2026.

This market will resolve to the country that receives the highest number of points from the professional juries in the Grand Final of the Eurovision Song Contest 2026.

All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket's Eurovision 2026 Jury Winner market favors Australia at 26.5% implied probability, driven by Delta Goodrem's veteran vocal prowess and compositional depth in "Eclipse," which has dominated pre-rehearsal Wiwi Jury polls and bookie odds since its March release. France trails closely at 21.5% with Monroe's elegant ballad appealing to juries' taste for sophistication, while Denmark (19.5%) benefits from historical jury reliability and Finland (16.0%) rides national final momentum but risks televote dilution. With top contenders neck-and-neck, rehearsals kicking off this week in Vienna could swing sentiment via staging and live delivery, as juries prioritize technical execution over spectacle ahead of the May 16 Grand Final.

The primary resolution source will be the official results published by the PGA Tour website (https://www.pgatour.com/).

events.title: PGA Tour: ONEflight Myrtle Beach Classic Top 5
events.description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed player finishes in the top 5 at the 2026 ONEflight Myrtle Beach Classic tournament, including ties. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". If final results are not announced by May 16, 2026 at 8:00PM ET this market will resolve to "No". eventMetadata.context_description: Mark Hubbard seized the lead at 16-under after a brilliant 8-under 64 in Round 3 of the ONEflight Myrtle Beach Classic at Dunes Golf & Beach Club, vaulting one stroke ahead of Aaron Rai (15-under). Kevin Roy holds third at 14-under, while Mac Meissner, Beau Hossler, and Brandt Snedeker share fourth at 13-under—positioning them strongly for top-5 finishes entering the final round. Brooks Koepka rocketed up 15 spots to 11-under with his own 64, lurking five back as a major threat amid birdie-friendly scoring conditions post-early rain. Limited course history in this PGA Tour opposite-field event amplifies the impact of recent form, tee-to-green accuracy, and wind management on par-72 layout for Sunday contention.

markets.question: Will David Njoku play for Minnesota Vikings in 2026-27?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the next team David Njoku officially joins by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If David Njoku does not officially join a new team by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If David Njoku joins a team that is not listed, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If David Njoku is released, retires, or is not under contract with any professional team by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

An official signing announcement prior to the market’s close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official announcements from the NFL and/or the acquiring team. A consensus of credible media reporting may also be used to resolve the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Free agent tight end David Njoku remains unsigned after the 2026 NFL Draft, with his February Instagram post confirming no return to the Browns after nine seasons dropping Cleveland to 24.5% trader consensus. Recent visits to the Chargers last week and Ravens in March have boosted those teams to 47.5% and 46.5%, but Bengals (48.4%), Falcons (47.9%), Eagles (47.4%), and others cluster tightly around 47% implied probability amid widespread TE needs, ample cap space, and schemes favoring pass-catching tight ends like Njoku's 4,000+ career yards. Post-draft roster gaps and Njoku's Pro Bowl production create a fluid, competitive market with no dominant frontrunner.

markets.question: Will Rocket Lab USA, Inc. (RKLB) hit (LOW) $76 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Rocket Lab USA, Inc. (RKLB) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Rocket Lab USA, Inc. (RKLB) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.RKLB%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.RKLB%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Rocket Lab USA, Inc. (RKLB) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will Rocket Lab USA, Inc. (RKLB) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Rocket Lab USA (RKLB) shares rocketed to a record $105.47 close on May 8, 2026—up over 30% intraday—fueled by Q1 revenue of $200.3 million, a 63.5% year-over-year surge that beat consensus, with space systems at $136.7 million and launches at $63.7 million, alongside a record $2.2 billion backlog and 38.2% GAAP gross margins. A landmark multi-launch contract for five Neutron and three Electron missions through 2029, plus a $30 million hypersonic deal with Anduril, amplified bullish trader sentiment on Neutron's Q4 2026 debut trajectory. Q2 guidance projects $225-240 million revenue; analysts' consensus price target averages $90, with highs to $120, as ongoing Electron cadence and Neutron milestones loom before May's close.

markets.question: Will the US acquire part of Greenland in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the United States acquires control of any land territory that is part of Greenland by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Only the transfer of sovereignty, or the acquisition of primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control qualifies.

1. Transfer of Sovereignty: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument results in a defined area of Greenland coming under the formal sovereignty of the U.S. (e.g., incorporated as a U.S. state, territory, possession, or other U.S. political classification), even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline.

2. Acquisition of Primary or Exclusive Jurisdiction or Control: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument establishes a defined area in Greenland in which the U.S. has primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over the territory, such that the ordinary legal authority of Denmark and Greenland do not apply,except by U.S. permission. Such agreements or instruments will qualify even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline. 

3. Use of Force: If the U.S. acquires primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over a defined area of Greenland through force, this will also qualify. 

An announcement will qualify only if it is accompanied by or consists of a binding agreement or legal instrument  (e.g., enacted legislation, a signed treaty, the signed text of an agreement, or an executive action implementing such an agreement) that unambiguously creates a transfer of sovereignty, or primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control,  even if this transfer or acquisition takes effect after the market deadline. 

Non-binding statements, negotiations, proposals, frameworks, or MOUs will not alone qualify. Basing rights, access agreements, SOFA-type arrangements, COFA-type arrangements, commercial concessions, or other permissions to use land (including leases) will not alone qualify. Any qualifying U.S. jurisdiction or control  in Greenland that existed at market creation will not count as new qualifying control.

Examples of qualifying events include but are not limited to treaty or piece of legislation that makes any portion of Greenland a U.S. territory or possession, even if the handover date for such territory or possession is later); or, a Guantánamo-style arrangement establishing a defined zone in Greenland under exclusive or primary U.S. jurisdiction and control, where Denmark and Greenland’s ordinary legal authority does not apply except by U.S. permission.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States, Denmark, and Greenland; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no U.S. acquisition of Greenland territory in 2026, driven by repeated firm rejections from Denmark and Greenlandic leaders affirming sovereignty as non-negotiable, echoing historical precedents like the 1941 Defense Agreement. President Trump's early-year push for national security control in the Arctic—citing China and Russia threats—sparked diplomatic talks and a vague "framework" announcement in January, but these evolved into military basing discussions without sovereignty transfer, as confirmed in April White House updates on "good trajectory" negotiations. A fringe U.S. citizen's May signature drive offering payments was dismissed as "indecent," underscoring local opposition amid NATO alliance strains, with no legislative progress on annexation bills leaving substantial diplomatic and legal barriers intact.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: Election results from New Jersey's 11th Congressional District special election on April 16 show Democrat Analilia Mejia defeating Republican Joe Hathaway 60%-39%, a 21-point margin aligning with trader consensus pricing Mejia's 20-25% victory bin at 97%. This commanding position stems from Mejia's outperformance of 2024 Democratic benchmarks in Hispanic-majority towns like Dover and flips of 13 Harris-lost municipalities, bolstered by higher Democratic turnout in the low-participation contest to replace Gov. Mikie Sherrill. Final tallies reported by outlets like the New York Times (>95% counted) and New Jersey Globe confirm stability since April 17, with canvassing complete by early May. Realistic challenges—late provisional ballots or a GOP-requested recount—face steep hurdles given the wide lead.

markets.question: Will the Communist Party of the Russian Federation (KPRF) win the most seats in the next Russian parliamentary election?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are to be scheduled to be held in Russia in September 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Russian State Duma election.

If the results are not known definitively by September 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the State Duma of the Federal Assembly of the Russian Federation.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by Russian government sources such as the Central Election Commission of the Russian Federation.

Note: If a listed party contests the election as part of a jointly listed coalition with one or more parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the listed party will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If two or more listed parties contest the election as a jointly listed coalition, with or without additional parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the party within that coalition that won the most seats in the previous election will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If such a coalition dissolves, each market will again represent its respective party individually.

eventMetadata.context_description: United Russia's 95.5% implied probability for most seats in the September 18–20, 2026, State Duma elections reflects its entrenched supermajority (315 of 450 current seats), advantages in the parallel voting system—225 party-list proportional seats above a 5% threshold and 225 single-member constituencies—and Kremlin preparations including redistricting, expanded electronic voting, and nominations of war veterans like those from the "Time of Heroes" program. Recent April polls (FOM: 37–39%; WCIOM: 28%) show United Russia leading lists ahead of LDPR (10%) and New People (up to 13%), with opposition parties facing leadership crises, arrests, and limited grassroots amid Putin’s April calls for unity against destabilization. Challenges would require unlikely surges from KPRF or New People, mass protests, or economic shocks disrupting administrative control.

markets.question: Bank of England decreases interest rates by 50+ bps after July 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in basis points in the Bank Rate resulting from the July 2026 meeting of the Bank of England’s Monetary Policy Committee, relative to the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source will be official information from the Bank of England, including the statement or release from its July 2026 Monetary Policy Committee meeting, scheduled for July 30, 2026, as listed on the official Bank of England calendar (https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/monetary-policy/upcoming-mpc-dates). This market may resolve as soon as the statement or release of the Bank of England's July 2026 Monetary Policy Committee meeting with relevant data is issued.

If the specified rate is defined by an upper and lower bound, the relevant change will be the change to the upper bound.

If the specified rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded according to the following guidelines. Increases or decreases of less than 25 bps will be rounded to 25 bps (e.g. an increase or decrease of 10 bps would be considered to be an increase or decrease of 25 bps). Increases or decreases of greater than 25 bps will be rounded to the nearest 25 bps and will be rounded away from 0 in cases of equidistance (e.g., an increase or decrease of 37.5 bps would be considered to be an increase or decrease of 50 bps). Displayed options of “Increase” or “Decrease” will include policy rate increases or decreases of any size.

If the specified meeting is postponed to a date and time before the start of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve based on the outcome of that postponed meeting. If the specified meeting is cancelled, or postponed such that no decision is announced by the start of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the “No Change” bracket. Emergency changes to the specified rate not resulting from the specified meeting will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price evenly matched implied probabilities of 49.5% across all Bank of England July Monetary Policy Committee outcomes—from 25 basis points or 50+ bps hikes/cuts to no change—reflecting deep uncertainty over the rate path amid conflicting signals. March 2026 CPI accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year from 3.0%, fueled by energy and motor fuel spikes from Middle East conflict, leading to a unanimous MPC hold at 3.75% Bank Rate. Resilient labor conditions, with unemployment falling to 4.9% in February, counterbalance disinflation forces, while persistent services inflation sustains hike risks. Pivotal catalysts include today's April 30 decision, upcoming May/June CPI data, and energy price developments ahead of the July 30 meeting.

markets.question: CS Independiente Rivadavia vs. CA Unión: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Primera División Argentina game between CS Independiente Rivadavia and CA Unión, scheduled for May 9 at 8:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if CS Independiente Rivadavia and CA Unión combine to score 3 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 3, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on afa.com.ar. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: CS Independiente Rivadavia vs. CA Unión - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Primera División Argentina game, scheduled for May 9 at 8:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Independiente Rivadavia tops Group B in the Liga Profesional Argentina after a crucial 2-0 away victory over Tigre last weekend, extending their strong run with 10 wins in 16 matches and a +14 goal difference in the annual table, boosting trader focus on their home advantage at Estadio Bautista Gargantini. Unión Santa Fe, 8th in Group A, drew 2-2 in their September 2025 head-to-head but face travel from Santa Fe and absences including N. Palavecino (knee, late May) and B. Pitton (muscle). Independiente Rivadavia's R. Macagno remains doubtful (mid-May return), yet their recent form and standings edge shape market sentiment in this closely contested matchup.

markets.question: Santos: Hernan Casanova vs Franco Roncadelli
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Hernan Casanova and Franco Roncadelli in the Santos, originally scheduled for May 10, 2026 at 9:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Hernan Casanova' if Hernan Casanova advances against Franco Roncadelli.

This market will resolve to 'Franco Roncadelli' if Franco Roncadelli advances against Hernan Casanova.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the ATP Challenger Santos final on clay, 32-year-old Argentine Hernan Casanova (ATP 420) faces second seed Franco Roncadelli (ATP 274) of Uruguay, with Casanova chasing his first Challenger title in his second career final. Casanova advanced with straight-sets wins over Nicolas Kicker (6-3, 7-5), Murkel Dellien (6-2, 6-0), and junior Luis Guto Miguel (6-3, 6-4) in the semis on May 9. Roncadelli rallied past Matheus Pucinelli de Almeida 1-6, 6-3, 6-4 in the other semi after earlier victories including Conner Huertas del Pino (7-6(5), 6-2). Casanova holds a 2-0 head-to-head edge on clay, most recently in Buenos Aires qualifiers last September, underscoring a competitive matchup shaped by recent momentum and surface familiarity.

markets.question: No change in Bank of Japan’s interest rates after the July 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
The Statement on Monetary Policy for the Bank of Japan's Monetary Policy meeting for July is scheduled to be released on July 31, 2026 (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm).

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the short-term policy interest rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Bank of Japan's July 2026 meeting.

If the short-term policy interest rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Bank of Japan (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Japan's statement for the specified meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the WI-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the WI-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Republican Glenn Grothman’s strong reelection bid in Wisconsin’s 6th Congressional District, rated Solid Republican by Cook Political Report and Safe Republican by Sabato’s Crystal Ball, drives trader consensus heavily toward the GOP at 82.5%. The district’s R+8 Cook Partisan Voter Index and Grothman’s history of double-digit general election margins—61% in 2024—bolster this positioning, amid a fragmented Democratic primary featuring eight candidates, none with significant fundraising to challenge his $700,000 cash on hand as of late March. Recent FEC filings highlight Bradley Smith’s modest grassroots momentum among Democrats, but no polls exist yet in this early cycle ahead of the June 1 filing deadline and August 11 primaries. Independents like firefighter Michael Thurow add minor uncertainty, though structural advantages favor Republicans absent major shifts.

markets.question: Will the Philadelphia 76ers win the NBA Eastern Conference Finals?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Philadelphia 76ers win the 2025–2026 NBA Eastern Conference Finals. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2025–26 NBA Eastern Conference Finals based on the rules of the NBA.

If the 2025-26 NBA Eastern Conference Finals winner is not announced by June 30, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

events.title: NBA Playoffs: Eastern Conference Champion
events.description: This is a market on which team will win the Eastern Conference Finals in the 2025–26 NBA season.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors the New York Knicks at 57% implied probability to claim the Eastern Conference championship, driven by their dominant 3-0 series lead over the No. 7 Philadelphia 76ers in the conference semifinals, capped by Jalen Brunson's 33-point outing in Game 3's 108-94 victory that positions them one win from the East Finals. The Detroit Pistons sit at 32% after taking a 2-1 edge versus the No. 4 Cleveland Cavaliers, bolstered by their gritty 4-3 first-round comeback from a 3-1 deficit against the Magic and Cade Cunningham's commanding play, though a 116-109 Game 3 road loss tempers momentum. The Cavaliers' 8.5% reflects upset potential via home-court resilience, while the 76ers' 0.4% underscores their brink-of-elimination status.

markets.question: Will Africa win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the continent of the country that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup, currently scheduled for June 11-July 19, 2026.

For example, if France wins the tournament, the market will resolve to Europe.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The definitive source of the continent for each country will be World Population Review (https://worldpopulationreview.com/country-rankings/list-of-countries-by-continent).

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/) and the World Population Review; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

For example, if France wins the tournament, the market will resolve to Europe.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The definitive source of the continent for each country will be World Population Review (https://worldpopulationreview.com/country-rankings/list-of-countries-by-continent).

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/) and the World Population Review; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Europe at 71.5% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup, driven by the UEFA confederation's 16 qualified teams including top power rankings leaders Spain, France, England, Germany, Portugal, and Netherlands, bolstered by late March playoff triumphs for Bosnia and Herzegovina, Sweden, Türkiye, and Czechia over higher seeds like Italy and Poland. South America's 20.5% reflects its six strong qualifiers—Argentina, Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador, Paraguay, Uruguay—with defending champions Argentina maintaining elite form post-Copa América. Africa (3.5%) and Asia (3.4%) benefit from expanded slots (10 and 9 teams) like Morocco, Senegal, Japan, and South Korea, but lack proven knockout pedigree; North America (2.5%) leans on hosts USA, Canada, Mexico amid modest group draws, while Oceania (0.3%) hinges on New Zealand's minnow status. Final qualifiers in March locked the 48-team field ahead of the June 11 kickoff.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ralph Alvarado's commanding 93% implied probability in the Kentucky 6th Congressional District Republican primary stems from President Trump's May 1 endorsement, labeling him a "true friend to MAGA," which has consolidated support in the open seat race vacated by Rep. Andy Barr's Senate bid. Additional backing from the U.S. Chamber of Commerce in late April and Rep. Brett Guthrie, coupled with Alvarado's fundraising dominance and recent super PAC spending, have widened his lead ahead of the May 19 closed primary. Challengers like Gavin Solomon trail far behind amid limited visibility. While odds exceed 90%, a late scandal, superior turnout by underdogs, or unexpected endorsements could narrow the gap in this final sprint.

markets.question: Will New Slovenia – Christian Democrats (NSi) be part of the next Government of Slovenia?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed political party is included in the first Slovenian government formed after the 2026 Slovenian parliamentary elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following a successful confirmation vote in the Slovenian National Assembly (Državni zbor), with ministers sworn in under non-caretaker circumstances.

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the Government of Slovenia.

events.title: Which parties will be part of the next Government of Slovenia?
events.description:

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed political party is included in the first Slovenian government formed after the 2026 Slovenian parliamentary elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following a successful vote of confidence in the Slovenian National Assembly (Državni zbor), with ministers sworn in under non-caretaker circumstances.

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the Government of Slovenia.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Slovenia's March 22, 2026, parliamentary elections, no party secured a National Assembly majority of 46 seats in the 90-seat chamber, yielding a hung parliament: Freedom Movement (GS) with 29, Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) with 28, NSi-SLS-Fokus alliance with 9, Democrats (DEM) and Social Democrats (SD) at 6 each, The Left (Levica) and Resni.ca at 5 each. Incumbent PM Robert Golob's broad coalition bid collapsed April 20 after rejections from centre-right groups, leading President Nataša Pirc Musar to decline a prime minister mandate April 25 due to insufficient support. Early May developments show SDS leader Janez Janša advancing minority government talks with NSi-SLS-Fokus and DEM (43 seats total), eyeing Resni.ca external votes; failure risks snap elections or prolonged deadlock.

markets.question: Will Natural Gas (NG) hit (LOW) $2.00 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of Natural Gas futures has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For Natural Gas futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For Natural Gas (NG) futures, the last trading day is defined as the third last business day of the month prior to the contract month, consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month Natural Gas futures "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=NGD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published for the Active Month Natural Gas (NG) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Natural Gas (NG) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will Natural Gas (NG) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Henry Hub natural gas spot prices hovered around $2.67 per million British thermal units (MMBtu) as of early May 2026, with June futures settling near $2.75/MMBtu in mild contango signaling anticipated summer cooling demand. The primary catalyst bolstering trader sentiment was the EIA's latest weekly storage report showing a smaller-than-expected +63 billion cubic feet (Bcf) injection for the week ending May 1, to 2,205 Bcf total—below consensus forecasts of 72-74 Bcf—reflecting resilient LNG export demand that hit monthly records despite mild weather curbing domestic consumption. U.S. marketed production is projected to rise 2% this year to 120.8 Bcf/d, tempered by Permian pipeline constraints, while exports could average 17 Bcf/d. Key near-term catalysts include weekly EIA storage updates (next on May 15) and extended summer weather outlooks, which could tighten balances if heat accelerates power burn.

Cremonese's recent 0-0 draw at Torino extended their winless streak to four amid defensive resilience but attacking woes, exacerbated by injuries to striker Jamie Vardy (muscle, late April doubt) and Morten Thorsby (calf). Pisa slumped to a 1-2 home defeat against Genoa last weekend, winless in five with dismal away form (no victories in 17 road games), missing midfielder Marius Marin (knee) and defender Daniel Denoon (ankle). Pisa hold a recent head-to-head edge, winning 1-0 in November, but Cremonese's home advantage and desperation could shift trader consensus in this survival decider.

markets.question: Will SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12 launch by May 31?
markets.description:

The outcome of any launch will be corroborated by examining official video provided by SpaceX (https://www.youtube.com/c/SpaceX), as well as secondary video feeds and/or written reports if necessary.

events.title: SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12
events.description: This is a market on the outcome of the SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12.

eventMetadata.context_description: SpaceX has accelerated preparations for Starship Flight Test 12, the debut of Block 3 hardware from Orbital Launch Pad 2, with FAA advisories opening a launch window as early as May 12, 2026, and extending through mid-May. Key catalysts include Booster 19's successful full-thrust 33-engine static fire on May 8 and Ship 39's static fire at Massey's, followed by Quick Disconnect hookup for wet dress rehearsal—despite an initial propellant load abort, a retry is underway. These milestones reflect SpaceX's rapid iteration post-Flight 11, enhancing reliability for the reusable launch vehicle amid minimal regulatory friction. Traders eye WDR completion, full stack testing, and final FAA clearance as swing factors, with historical precedents suggesting potential slips from technical glitches but strong momentum toward liftoff.

French hopefuls Arthur Fils (2.9%) and Lorenzo Musetti (2.4%) draw modest home-crowd support, but Sinner's 28-match Masters 1000 streak underscores the field's steep challenge on best-of-five clay.

events.title: UFC 328: Jeremy Stephens vs. King Green (Lightweight, Main Card)

eventMetadata.context_description: Jeremy Stephens' dramatic weight miss at 160 pounds—four over the lightweight limit—turned his UFC comeback bout against King Green into a catchweight affair at UFC 328, with Stephens forfeiting 30% of his purse and likely entering dehydrated and compromised. The 39-year-old veteran, absent from the Octagon since May 2025 after personally lobbying Dana White for the slot, faces an active Green (34-17-1), who notched recent wins over Daniel Zellhuber and Lance Gibson Jr. amid sharp boxing and submission threats. Traders weighed Green's momentum, stylistic edges in reach and grappling against Stephens' knockout power and rust, amplifying favoritism amid the main card opener at Prudential Center.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will US withdraw from NATO by...?
events.description:

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes".

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Pentagon announcements of a 5,000-troop withdrawal from Germany, potentially expanding to other sites like Italy, have heightened transatlantic tensions, stemming from disputes over ally support amid the Iran conflict, tariff policies, and denied basing rights for U.S. operations. President Trump's public reevaluation of U.S. NATO membership echoes longstanding complaints about burden-sharing, prompting European leaders like Germany's defense minister to call for greater continental self-reliance. However, the 2024 National Defense Authorization Act (Section 1250A) bars unilateral withdrawal without a two-thirds Senate vote or congressional legislation, imposing steep procedural hurdles. Traders price low odds on exit due to these structural barriers, with upcoming White House-NATO Secretary General Rutte talks as a key watchpoint for de-escalation signals.

A crippling injury crisis—featuring defender Miro Muheim's season-ending ankle issue, center-back Luka Vuskovic's knee bruise, midfielder Albert Sambi Lokonga, forward Yussuf Poulsen's hamstring strain, and others—exposes defensive frailties against Freiburg's 8th-placed side (12-7-11), who won 2-1 at home in January. Freiburg contends with knee surgery for Patrick Osterhage and hamstring problems for Max Rosenfelder, but their average away record and HSV's woes suggest trader consensus for a low-scoring, competitive affair.

markets.question: Will ByteDance have the best Coding AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab for "Coding" is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Coding" Leaderboard tab at https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/coding-no-style-control with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie still remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

While OpenAI's GPT-5.5 shows gains in speed and full-stack work, it trails on plan quality and precision. eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Mayor Mark Sutcliffe holds trader consensus at 62.5% implied probability for the October 26, 2026, Ottawa municipal election, buoyed by name recognition, a seasoned campaign apparatus, and a fragmented opposition field reminiscent of his 2022 victory amid split challengers. A Liaison Strategies poll from mid-April (n=1,000) showed him leading decided voters 46%-37% over Kitchissippi Coun. Jeff Leiper—whose 19.5% market share reflects his progressive profile and early filing on May 1—but with 48% undecided overall and net negative approvals on transit (71% disapprove), housing affordability (54% disapprove), and homelessness. Homebuilder Alex Lawson (9.3%) and economist Neil Saravanamuttoo (5.8%), both filing May 1, trail as niche candidates, while former contender Catherine McKenney lags at 3.1%; nominations close August 21, with key issues centering housing, transit reliability, and cost of living.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the 2027 French presidential election first round set for April 11 or 18, incumbent Emmanuel Macron ineligible due to term limits, a fragmented field has emerged amid ongoing political instability from the hung National Assembly since 2024 snap legislative elections. Radical left leader Jean-Luc Mélenchon declared his fourth candidacy on May 3, polarizing the left where a primary is slated for October 11—though opposed by LFI and others—while Les Républicains designated Bruno Retailleau on April 19 and minor figures like Nathalie Arthaud, François Asselineau, and Florian Philippot (May 9) have announced. Marine Le Pen's eligibility hangs on a July 7 Paris appeals court ruling on her embezzlement conviction, potentially paving the way for Jordan Bardella as RN frontrunner per recent polls. Édouard Philippe and Gabriel Attal lead center-right potentials without formal declarations; all require 500 elected official sponsorships by early 2027 to appear on the ballot. March municipal elections signaled far-right gains but no major city breakthroughs, fueling trader focus on primaries, legal outcomes, and senatorial votes in late September.

markets.question: Will Argentina’s annual inflation in 2026 be between 30% and 34.9%?
markets.description:
This is a market about Argentinian inflation over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the National Institute of Statistics and Census (INDEC) of Argentina.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI / IPC) over the 12-month period ending in December 2026 (Variación % interanual Total nacional) according to the monthly INDEC report.

The resolution source for this market will be the INDEC Consumer Price Index report released for December 2026 (https://www.indec.gob.ar/), expected to be released in January 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by clicking on the “Precios al consumidor” option on the home page of https://www.indec.gob.ar/, and searching the pdf for the figure under “Variación % interanual Total nacional”.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly INDEC CPI (IPC) news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 33.6%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 40.8% implied probability for Argentina's 2026 annual inflation landing in the 30.0-34.9% range, closely tracking the central bank's latest Relevamiento de Expectativas de Mercado (REM) survey from May 7, where analysts raised their median year-end forecast to 30.5%—up 1.4 percentage points—amid March's higher-than-expected 3.4% monthly CPI rise (32.6% YoY per INDEC), driven by transportation costs and seasonal education expenses. This uptick reversed prior disinflation momentum under President Milei's fiscal austerity, with the IMF also lifting its projection to 30.4%. Upcoming April CPI data, due mid-May, represents a key catalyst that could validate or shift these probabilities, as monthly rates above 2.5-3% would pressure the 25-29.9% bin (24.5%).

markets.question: Will François Hollande win the 2027 French presidential election?
markets.description:
The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held around April 2027.  This market pertains to the outcome of the next French presidential election, regardless of whether it follows the scheduled end of the current term or is held earlier. 

The President of France is elected via a two-round system; a candidate must secure over 50% of the vote to win outright in the first round. If no candidate achieves this, the top two contenders advance to a runoff.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If, for any reason, the results of the election are not known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

markets.question: Gemini 3.2 released by May 22, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Google's Gemini 3.2 model is made available to the general public by the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

Gemini 3.2 refers to a product explicitly named Gemini 3.2, or a variant that is recognized as a direct successor to Gemini 3.1, similar to the progression from Gemini 3 to Gemini 3.1. (e.g., Gemini 3.2 GA, Gemini 3.2/3.3/3.4, etc., in any variant, like Pro/Deep Think/Flash/Flash-lite, would qualify toward a "Yes" resolution to this market).

Specialized models for non-text modalities such as video generation (e.g., Veo), image generation (e.g., Imagen, Nano Banana), music generation (e.g., Lyria), or robotics (e.g., Gemini Robotics) will NOT qualify, even if released under the Gemini 3.2 version number. Products labeled as a GA promotion of an already-existing Preview model (e.g., Gemini 3.1 GA) or a new flagship generation (e.g., Gemini 4) or similar that are not explicitly labeled as described above will NOT qualify.

A qualifying model must be launched and publicly accessible, including via open beta or open rolling waitlist signups. A closed beta or any form of private access will not suffice. The release must be either clearly defined and publicly announced by Google as being accessible to the general public or otherwise made publicly accessible and explicitly labeled within the company's official website. Labeling errors, placeholder text, or version names displayed on the website that do not correspond to a model that is actually accessible to the general public will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Google, with additional verification from a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will Joe Flacco start Week 1 for the Chiefs in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who is the starting quarterback for the Kansas City Chiefs in their Week 1 regular season game of the 2026-2027 NFL season.

If the Chiefs do not name a single player as the starting quarterback, the market will resolve based on the player who takes the most snaps at quarterback during their first offensive drive of the game. If multiple players take the same number of snaps on the Chiefs’ first offensive drive, the market will resolve to the player who took the first snap at quarterback.

This market may not resolve before the start of the Chiefs’ Week 1 regular season game. 

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the NFL (NFL.com); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Patrick Mahomes at 58.5% implied probability to start Week 1 for the Kansas City Chiefs in 2026, but Gardner Minshew trails closely at 52.5%, reflecting deep uncertainty over Mahomes' recovery from a late-2025 torn ACL that sidelined him. Andy Reid's recent OTA comments emphasized caution, noting Mahomes is throwing independently but could open training camp on the PUP list to protect his knee, keeping the door open for backups amid rapid QB depth changes. Justin Fields joined as a high-upside backup in March, while practice-squad riser Chris Oladokun (35.4%) and veterans Joe Flacco (28.1%) add intrigue, as no other signal-callers were initially contracted beyond Mahomes, fueling a competitive depth chart battle into the summer.

markets.question: Will Gold (GC) hit (HIGH) $5,700 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Gold (GC) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Gold (GC) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: COMEX gold futures (GC) for June delivery trade near $4,730 per ounce as of May 10, 2026, reflecting trader consensus on sustained upside potential driven by a softer U.S. dollar (DXY at 97.90) and Federal Reserve signals of potential rate cuts amid cooling inflation. Recent gains stem from U.S.-Iran diplomatic hopes boosting safe-haven flows alongside European ETF inflows and ongoing central bank accumulation, offsetting April's risk-on pressures. Key supports include negative real yields and geopolitical risks, with historical precedents showing gold resilience above $4,500 in low-rate environments. Watch May CPI (mid-June release) and FOMC June 16-17 meeting for policy pivots that could propel prices toward $5,000 or trigger pullbacks.

markets.question: Will inflation reach more than 5% in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Consumer Price Index (CPI) increased by greater than the listed percent over the 12 month period ending with any month in 2026 according to the monthly Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) reports. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index reports released for each month of 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm). Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the December 2026 report is issued. Once the December 2026 report is issued, any revisions to previously released CPI figures will not be counted toward this market's resolution. If the CPI report for December 2026 is not issued by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on CPI figures which have already been made available by the BLS.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly BLS CPI news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on peak 2026 inflation hinges on March data showing reacceleration, with CPI at 3.3% year-over-year—the highest since May 2024—and the Fed's preferred PCE at 3.5%, both exceeding prior months' 2.4-2.8% readings amid sticky core pressures and energy cost rebounds. The Federal Reserve's March 2026 dot plot raised median PCE inflation projections to 2.7% for year-end, up from 2.4%, reflecting policymaker caution despite holding rates steady at the April 28-29 FOMC meeting. Market-implied odds aggregate real-capital bets on trajectories above 3%, tempered by base rates from disinflation cycles. Key catalysts include tomorrow's April CPI release on May 12 and the June 16-17 FOMC, where hotter prints could push expectations toward 4%.

markets.question: Yoon out of custody before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President of South Korea Yoon Suk Yeol is released from custody by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Yoon is released but remains under house arrest, the market will still resolve to "Yes".

If Yoon is released on parole, bond, or any other condition that results in them leaving state custody, the market will resolve to "Yes".

Transporting Yoon to another location of custody (e.g., a different prison, court, or hospital within the correctional system) will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from government authorities or corrections departments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Former South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol remains in detention following his February 2026 life imprisonment sentence for leading an insurrection tied to his 2024 martial law declaration, after the Constitutional Court upheld his impeachment and removal from office in April 2025. Multiple convictions, including a five-year term for obstructing arrest (upheld and increased to seven years by an appeals court on April 29, 2026), reinforce trader consensus implying a 92.1% probability he stays in custody through 2026. Ongoing appeals in his insurrection trial, with hearings as recent as May 7, show no bail grants or release signals, amid procedural hurdles like extended detention warrants that historically prolong high-profile cases. Late-breaking acquittals or health-related pardons could shift odds, though none appear imminent.

markets.question: Will Crude Oil (CL) hit (LOW) $40 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Crude Oil (CL) futures is equal to or below the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Crude Oil (CL) futures contracts, the active month is the nearest of the contract months listed. The active month becomes a non-active month effective two business days prior to the spot month expiration. For example; if the spot month expires on a Friday the next listed contract will be considered the Active Month on the Wednesday prior to the spot month expiration.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil (CL) front-month futures settled at $95.42 per barrel on May 8, 2026, up 0.64% amid renewed Middle East tensions driving a 1.11% intraday gain to around $95.80, offsetting earlier pullbacks from de-escalating Strait of Hormuz risks. Trader consensus reflects caution on supply dynamics, with OPEC+ set to cut output by 188,000 barrels per day in June and EIA inventories showing persistent draws amid robust global demand signals from China. Polymarket positions aggregate skin-in-the-game views pricing elevated volatility through June settlement.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the TX-38 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the TX-38 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Texas's 38th Congressional District, a Republican stronghold in Houston suburbs, drives trader consensus at 83.5% for a Republican House winner following incumbent Wesley Hunt's departure to challenge for U.S. Senate. The March 3 Republican primary produced no majority winner among 10 candidates, advancing Trump-endorsed mortgage banker Jon Bonck and airport executive Shelly deZevallos to the May 26 runoff to select the GOP nominee. Democrat Melissa McDonough secured her party's nomination unopposed. With no competitive general election polling and the district's historical GOP dominance—rated Solid Republican by Cook Political Report—traders see significant barriers for Democrats absent major shifts like scandals or turnout surges in November.

markets.question: Will SBV Excelsior win on 2026-05-10?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 10, 2026
If SBV Excelsior wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: SBV Excelsior vs. FC Volendam
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Eredivisie game, scheduled for Sunday, May 10, 2026 between SBV Excelsior and FC Volendam.

eventMetadata.context_description: SBV Excelsior's trader consensus edge at 55.5% implied probability stems from home advantage at Stadion Woudestein and recent head-to-head dominance, winning the last two Eredivisie clashes 2-1 and 4-1 while unbeaten in four meetings. Excelsior sit 14th, one spot above 15th-placed FC Volendam in a tight relegation scrap, with mixed recent form including a 5-0 thrashing of FC Utrecht but losses to Feyenoord and NEC; they've netted nine goals across their last five outings versus Volendam's four amid mostly defeats. Minor injuries sideline Excelsior's Jonathans and Akujobi, Volendam's Kwakman, keeping the draw at 29% and visitors viable at 28% given both sides' inconsistent away/home records and low-table volatility.

markets.question: Will Marco Rubio win the 2028 US Presidential Election?
markets.description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

California Governor Gavin Newsom holds steady at 16.7% as the Democratic frontrunner in an open field, despite fresh conservative media attacks on his wife. With 2026 midterms looming, outcomes in swing states, Trump approval trends, and economic recovery could widen gaps among these closely contested leaders.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democrat April McClain Delaney leads trader consensus at 87.5% implied probability to win Maryland's 6th Congressional District House seat, reflecting the district's Safe Democratic rating from Sabato's Crystal Ball and Cook Political Report amid a partisan lean favoring Democrats by wide margins in recent cycles. A Public Sentiment Institute poll from April 8-11 showed Delaney at 55% against a generic Republican at 31%, with challenger David Trone polling even stronger at 59%, underscoring Democratic dominance regardless of the June 23 primary outcome between the heavily self-funded rivals. Weak Republican fundraising—top GOP candidates under $310,000 raised—and historical incumbency advantages in this suburban-rural district sustain low odds for Republicans at 9%, though national midterm dynamics could introduce volatility ahead of November 3.

markets.question: Will Trump’s approval rating be at least 40.0 on May 15, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to Silver Bulletin's approval rating for Donald Trump on May 15, 2026.

Note that the approval ratings for this date must be finalized before it is considered for this market (namely, once the next data point is available, the previous one is finalized).

This market's resolution source will be Silver Bulletin's approval rating poll aggregator, https://www.natesilver.net/p/trump-approval-ratings-nate-silver-bulletin, specifically the approval rating indicated by the green trend line for the resolution date. Changes in the methodology by which Silver Bulletin calculates the approval rating will have no bearing on the resolution of this market. If Silver Bulletin's approval rating becomes permanently unavailable, RealClearPolitics will be used.

The resolution source reports the rating value to only one decimal point (e.g., 42.8%, 33.9%, etc.). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

markets.question: Will the fight be won by submission?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the fight between Grant Dawson and Mateusz Rebecki at UFC 328: Chimaev vs. Strickland, scheduled for May 9, 2026, ends by submission, including tapout, verbal submission, or technical submission (referee stoppage due to a submission hold). Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

If the fight ends in a draw or by disqualification (either fighter), it will resolve "No."

If the bout is ruled a No Contest, not scored, canceled, or postponed beyond May 23, 2026, this market will resolve "50-50."

eventMetadata.context_description: Grant Dawson entered UFC 328's lightweight prelims as trader consensus favorite against Mateusz Rebecki, buoyed by his elite wrestling base, reach advantage, and submission threat honed after a recent loss that spurred camp improvements. Rebecki, riding a reputation for savage striking and finishes but coming off back-to-back decision setbacks—including a majority decision defeat to Ludovit Klein at UFC 321—faced stylistic questions in grappling exchanges. Both hit the lightweight limit cleanly at Friday's weigh-ins with no reported injuries or withdrawals, heightening focus on Dawson's control versus Rebecki's knockout power at Newark's Prudential Center. The bout highlighted Dawson's experience edge in a competitive division matchup.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve has held the federal funds target range steady at 3.50%-3.75%—with an effective rate of 3.64% as of May 9—for its third consecutive FOMC meeting in late April 2026, amid a split 8-4 vote signaling internal debate on policy stance. April nonfarm payrolls added 115,000 jobs, beating expectations, while unemployment held at 4.3%, underscoring labor market resilience that has curbed rate cut anticipation. CME FedWatch Tool-implied probabilities show less than 50% odds of cuts before late 2027, with markets pricing a modest rise toward 3.79% amid sticky inflation signals and geopolitical risks. Traders eye the June 16-17 FOMC and May CPI data as pivotal catalysts for any shifts.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Paris be 9°C on May 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded at the Paris-Le Bourget Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 10 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Paris-Le Bourget Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/fr/bonneuil-en-france/LFPB.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.
